channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Samsung has launched a flexible portable monitor complete with a briefcase, one that seems to come straight from a James Bond movie
item.description: Samsung unveils a foldable OLED monitor that transforms into a briefcase, combining portability with high-resolution display technology.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Authorities use high tech to enhance border security
item.description: Surveillance cameras and drones give border officials an advantage; but the challenge of data-sharing silos remains.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Google TV's Gemini Live support and other updates seemingly confirmed by new user survey – here's what to expect
item.description: Google's latest survey gives some big hints about incoming improvements, including chatting with Gemini Live.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple makes one hardware change to the iPad Air that might be the best indicator of its true lightweight tablet intentions
item.description: With the new iPad Air, Apple's removed the product name off the back, and while Apple is not telling us why it made that change, it's a notable one.
item.dc:creator: lance.ulanoff@futurenet.com (Lance Ulanoff), TechRadar

item.title: Tariffs for now won’t impact IT organizations
item.description: The impact of tariffs by the Trump Administration on semiconductors from Taiwan is so far not cause for alarm, according to one analyst.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 might improve on its predecessor in one crucial way
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 could come with a larger battery than the current-gen Galaxy Z Flip 6, a welcome improvement for any folding phone.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5070 early pricing hints at plenty of GPUs at the MSRP – but I’ll believe it when I see it
item.description: Nvidia RTX 5070 GPUs appear at MSRP in apparent early glimmers of hopeful pricing – but I really wouldn’t read much into this.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Another iconic Disney Castle is getting the Lego treatment, and this one roars
item.description: Making its debut at the 2025 Toy Fair alongside the Lego Ideas River Steamboat and a massive Lego T-Rex – is the Lego Disney Beauty and the Beast Castle building set.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Sony is now issuing PS Plus compensation following the recent PSN outage
item.description: PlayStation Plus subscribers are officially receiving compensation following the PSN outage last month.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Salesforce’s AgentExchange targets AI agent adoption, monetization
item.description: Salesforce is launching a new marketplace named AgentExchange for its agents and agent-related actions, topics, and templates to increase adoption of AI agents and allow its partners to monetize them.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The world’s thinnest phone was just revealed, but a new iPhone 17 Air leak suggests it could be even slimmer
item.description: Tecno has revealed its new Spark Slim smartphone at MWC 2025, and at 5.75mm thin it’s got a solid claim to the title of the world’s thinnest slab phone – but a new iPhone 17 Air leak suggests it could be even thinner.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Web DDoS attacks see major surge as AI allows more powerful attacks
item.description: Layer 7 Web DDoS attacks increased five-fold in the span of a year as hacktivists use AI to lower the barrier to entry.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Is your disaster recovery a house of cards? Why BIA, BCP, and DRP are your foundation
item.description: Disaster recovery is more than just an “IT issue.” In fact, successful recovery from cyberattacks and other disasters hinges on an approach that integrates business impact assessments (BIA), business continuity planning (BCP), and disaster recovery planning (DRP) including rigorous testing.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Electronic employee monitoring reaches an all-time high
item.description: Monitoring of both remote and in-office employees is at an all-time high, a trend highlighted by federal workers being told to report their weekly accomplishments. A study by the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) found that 80% of companies are monitoring remote or hybrid workers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Rockstar acquires studio behind Grand Theft Auto: The Trilogy - The Definitive Edition upgrades and L.A. Noire re-releases
item.description: Rockstar Games has acquired Grand Theft Auto: The Trilogy - The Definitive Edition developer Video Games Deluxe.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Las ocho nuevas reglas del liderazgo en TI
item.description: El papel del director de informática o TI (CIO) sigue evolucionando y cambiando de forma muy rápida, al mismo ritmo, de hecho, que la transformación que experimenta la tecnología que este perfil gestiona y mantiene.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: IT leaders brace for the AI agent management challenge
item.description: With AI agents poised to take over significant portions of enterprise workflows, IT leaders will be faced with an increasingly complex challenge: managing them.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tariffs an 'expensive game to play': Powerteam International CEO
item.description: Tariffs are going to be an "expensive game to play," Bill Walsh, CEO and founder of Powerteam International, said.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s crypto reserve is a payoff for loyalists
item.description: President Donald Trump has found a new way to reward his supporters. On Sunday, Trump announced in a Truth Social post that he will issue an executive order creating a “Crypto Strategic Reserve” that will include Bitcoin, Ethereum, and three other cryptocurrencies. “I will make sure the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: Hegseth halts cyber operations against Russia: Reports
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has halted offensive cyber operations against Russia, according to media reports. The Pentagon chief ordered U.S. Cyber Command to pause offensive operations last week, CNN and NBC reported. The move comes amid what appears to be a broader shift in U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Watch: Trump investment announcement
item.description: President Trump on Monday afternoon announced that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) plans to spend $100 billion in U.S. chip manufacturing plants on Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Mozilla is under fire for its updated Firefox user agreement
item.description: Mozilla last week updated the Firefox user agreement — something that normally does not provoke strong reactions. This time, however, the changes led to a wave of criticism, because some of the wording can be interpreted as giving Mozilla free rein to do whatever it wants with users’ data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Breaking up Intel is the ‘dumbest idea’ around: Former CEO Craig Barett
item.description: In a blistering critique, former Intel CEO and Chairman Craig Barrett has strongly opposed breaking the company into separate design and foundry units, arguing that Intel’s recent technological resurgence positions it to challenge TSMC’s dominance in the semiconductor industry without corporate dism
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Who needs Google technology? Probably not you
item.description: Clearly Google is doing something right. Although Google Cloud’s revenue still lags AWS and Microsoft Azure, it’s growing much faster (albeit on a smaller base). But that’s not the real story of its growth. The story is that it’s growing at all.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Everything you need to know about the Oscars’ AI controversy
item.description: Sometimes, authenticity can be a film’s most special effect. It took months for Best Actress front-runner Mikey Madison to learn how to pole dance like the titular exotic dancer in Anora and for her fellow nominee Timothée Chalamet to passably play guitar as Bob Dylan in A Complete Unknown.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: President Trump announced Sunday that the U.S. would be creating a crypto reserve, to boost the growing industry.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE structure, authority emerge as biggest DC mystery under Trump
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) structure and authority has become a central mystery in the barrage of lawsuits seeking to stymie its operations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office
item.description: Thomas Shedd, the commissioner of Federal Acquisition Service, said the decision is part of the President’s Workforce Optimization Initiative executive order. The post After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: TrapC proposal to fix C/C++ memory safety
item.description: TrapC, a fork of the C language, is being developed as a potential solution for memory safety issues that have hindered the C and C++ languages. TrapC is a proposed C-language extension compiler intended to be implemented as a cybersecurity compiler for C and C++ code, said developer Robin Rowe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rust 1.85 arrives with long-awaited async closures
item.description: Rust 1.85, the latest version of the burgeoning language positioned for speed, safety, and ease of use, has arrived, with new capabilities including async closures. The release also constitutes a stabilization of the Rust 2024 edition of the language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to restore a deleted text message on iPhone
item.description: Accidentally deleting a text message on your iPhone or iPad is not a problem if you still need the message. Apple has a built-in way to recover it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
